Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The objective is to construct an accepting Petri net in Python using the pm4py library based on a specific insurance claim management process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Process steps 1 Claim Registration CR 2 Document Verification DV 3 Fraud Check FC 4 Claim Assessment CA 5 Approval AP 6 Payment P 7 Notification N 8 Closure CL

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Loops Additional steps After DV if documents are incomplete Re Document Submission RDS occurs After FC if doubts In depth Investigation II happens This can happen multiple times before proceeding to Claim Assessment CA

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If RDS happens it probably loops back to Document Verification

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means after FC place choice proceed to CA OR Transition II back to FC or place before FC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The first transition is C or CR Let us use C for Insurance Claim or just start with CR Let us include C then CR

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait often C is just the event name Let us add C just to be safe Actually let us look at the exact wording A customer files an Insurance Claim C after which the following steps take place I will create transitions C CR DV RDS FC II CA AP P N CL

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: If p3 has arcs to RDS and FC then it is a non deterministic choice or a place with two outgoing transitions That is fine

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If we are at p3 after Document Verification we can either go to RDS which takes us back to p2 before Document Verification or go to FC which does Fraud Check

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If we are at p4 after Fraud Check we can either go to II which takes us back to p3 after Document Verification or go to CA which does Claim Assessment

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If we strictly want a loop between FC and II we would need a place before FC and a place after FC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Using invisible transitions makes the routing unambiguous

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The model without invisible transitions is perfectly valid as a Petri net It is actually a standard way to represent sequences with loops in basic Petri nets

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait The claim must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold This implies Approval AP might be optional

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This definitely implies AP is conditional So CA to place then AP or skip AP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Let us add an invisible transition for skipping Approval

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If we add an arc from the place before approval directly to Payment then Payment would have two incoming places creating an and join and the transition would never fire with only one token

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: To merge the flow after an xor split we need an xor join In Petri nets an xor join is represented by a place that receives arcs from multiple transitions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The final structure uses source then C then Claim Registration then Document Verification then an RDS loop and a silent skip to before Fraud Check then Fraud Check then an II loop and a silent skip to before Claim Assessment then Claim Assessment then Approval or silent skip then Payment then Notification then Closure to sink

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let us verify this model A customer files an Insurance Claim then Claim Registration then Document Verification If documents are incomplete RDS loops back before DV If documents are complete skip RDS goes to before FC Fraud Check then if FC raises doubts II loops back before FC otherwise skip II goes to before CA then Claim Assessment then Approval or skip Approval then Payment then Notification then Closure

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The model is extremely clean standard and avoids the artifact of doing RDS after II or doing FC after RDS without DV

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Trace the token flow in the main happy path source to C to Claim Registration to Document Verification to skip RDS to Fraud Check to skip II to Claim Assessment to skip AP to Payment to Notification to Closure reaches final marking Correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Trace with RDS loop DV then RDS then back before DV then DV again Correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Trace with II loop FC then II then back before FC then FC again Correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The model is sound No deadlocks no livelocks properly completed
